semanticCore: Sand-dunes, curved tracks of sand, winding dunes, or structural arches

keyDerivations.meaning: Winding sand-dune, curved sand track, or arched ridge of desert sand

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَاذْكُرْ أَخَا عَادٍ إِذْ أَنْذَرَ قَوْمَهُ بِالْأَحْقَافِ (46:21): And remember the brother of 'Ad (Hud), when he warned his people in the sand-dunes (Al-Ahqaf).
discardedNoise: Ahqaf (a modern administrative region in Yemen).
summaryEn: The root refers to curved or winding sand dunes (Ahqaf), designating the geographical region of 'Ad, who lived among massive sand formations in southern Arabia.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الكثبان الرملية المنحنية الهلالية الملتوية في الصحراء (حقف)، واصفاً موطن عاد قوم هود (الأحقاف) حيث حلت بهم عقوبة الريح.
rawText: أَحْقَفُ A camel lank in the belly. (ISh, K.) احقوقف 12 , said of sand, and of the هِلَال [or moon when, being near the sun, it shows a narrow rim of light], (S, K,) and of the back, (K,) or of anything, such as the back of a camel, (L,) It was curved, bent, or winding, (S, L, K,) and long. (L, K.) حَاقِفٌ A gazelle [lying] bent together, or curled, (A 'Obeyd, S, Mgh, Msb, K,) like a حِقْف of sand, (Az, Sgh, K,) in his sleep, (A'Obeyd, S, K,) or in consequence of a wound &c.: (Msb:) or lying upon his breast on a حِقْف of sand: (IAar, K:) or, as some say, upon, or at, the lower, or lowest, part of a حِقْف. (Mgh.) حَقَفَ 1 , aor. ﹹ, inf.n. حُقُوفٌ, It (a thing) was, or became, curved, bent, or winding. (Msb.) حُقُوفٌ also signifies A gazelle's being in the condition denoted by [the part. n.] حَاقِفٌ in the phrase ظَبْىٌ حَاقِفٌ, which see below. (Ibn- 'Abbád, K.) حِقْفٌ A curving, or winding, tract of sand: (S, Mgh, Msb, K:) pl. [of pauc.] أَحْقَافٌ (S, Msb, K) and [of mult.] حِقَافٌ (S, K) and حُقُوفٌ, (O, L, K,) and pl. pl. حَقَائِفُ and حِقَفَةٌ, (K,) the former of these two being pl. of أَحْقَافٌ or of حِقَافٌ, (L, TA,) but the latter of them, accord. to the O, is a pl., not a pl. pl. : (TA:) or a heap, or hill, of sand, that has assumed a bowed form: (IDrd, TA:) or a curving, or winding, thin tract of sand: (Har p. 51:) or a great, round tract of sand: (Ibn-' Arafeh, K:) or an oblong, elevated tract of sand: (Fr, K:) or الأَحْقَافُ applies [particularly] to certain oblong tracts of sand in the region of Esh-Shihr: (K, * TA:) accord. to J, [in the S,] to the country of 'Ád. (TA.) === Also The lower, or lowest, part of a tract of sand, and of a mountain, and of a wall. (O, L, K.)
